title: The Pill Bottle on the Nightstand

story.opening: <break time="500ms"/>I took sleeping pills for eighteen months. <break time="400ms"/>I was ashamed of it the whole time.
story.body: <break time="450ms"/>There's this voice that says: <break time="400ms"/>'You should be able to do this naturally. <break time="350ms"/>Pills are a crutch. <break time="300ms"/>You're weak.' <break time="500ms"/>I listened to that voice. <break time="400ms"/>I'd take the pill and feel like a failure. <break time="350ms"/>Even as it gave me the only rest I'd had in months. <break time="600ms"/>My doctor said something I needed to hear: <break time="500ms"/>'If you needed insulin for diabetes, would you feel ashamed?' <break time="400ms"/>Sleep deprivation is a medical problem. <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes medicine is the answer.
story.reflection: <break time="600ms"/>I don't take them anymore. <break time="500ms"/>But I would if I needed to. <break time="400ms"/>Without shame. <break time="600ms"/>If you're using medication to sleep - <break time="500ms"/>that's not failure. <break time="400ms"/>That's treating a problem. <break time="350ms"/>That's surviving. <break time="600ms"/>The shame helps no one. <break time="500ms"/>Least of all you.

when_to_share: When someone expresses shame about using sleep medication
